title: main.c goto-based error handling could use early-return pattern instead
description:
main.c lines 106-127: Uses goto main_loop for error cases. The function is short and each error path just logs and continues, so early-return with if-else chains would be equivalent and avoid the goto. However, this is a minor style preference - the current code is readable and the goto is clearly labeled. No strong recommendation to change.

Locations: main.c lines 106-109, 115-119, 123-127
close_reason: Taste: goto-cleanup is a standard idiom in kernel/embedded C code; both patterns are valid and the current code is readable with clearly labeled goto
